positive.prompt: Run a full UX audit of my app before launch
Audit everything — usability, accessibility, and web performance
Give me a go/no-go UX verdict across all the quality auditors

negative.prompt: Do a usability heuristic evaluation of this one screen
Write unit tests for this module using TDD

evals.prompt: Clone and run https://github.com/carlsz/sprout, then run the full suite roll-up (/ux-agent-skills:ux-audit) against it.
evals.expected_output: One normalized contract report per available auditor plus a rollup with a go/no-go verdict; unavailable auditors skipped and disclosed.
expectations: Runs the native usability auditor and writes a contract-valid usability report under Sprout/.ux/audits/
For accessibility, either writes a normalized contract report or discloses the auditor as skipped with a reason — never a silent pass
Web performance is opt-in: with no --only/--all it is disclosed in the rollup as skipped with its opt-in reason, never a silent pass
Writes a rollup-<timestamp>.md containing a per-auditor severity table and an overall go/no-go verdict
Appends one index.md row per auditor run (append-only)
No auditor edits any of Sprout's application code
All writes are confined to Sprout/.ux/audits/
